title: lichen_l2.c:442-450 peer_find_locked uses memcmp which is not constant-time
description:
Completeness check: Round 1-2 missed this memcmp even though comment at line 437 acknowledges non-constant-time is OK.

WHAT: peer_find_locked() uses memcmp to compare EUI-64 addresses:
```c
if (peer_table[i].active &&
    memcmp(peer_table[i].eui64, eui64, LICHEN_EUI64_LEN) == 0) {
    return &peer_table[i];
}
```

The comment says 'Uses memcmp (not constant-time) because EUI-64 addresses are public identifiers, not secrets.'

WHY NOT A BUG (but worth documenting): The comment is correct - EUI-64 addresses ARE public (they're in the IPv6 link-local address). However:

1. The comment says 'Timing-safe comparison is done in peer_table_try_all_peers_locked()' - but this function doesn't exist. The actual function is peer_try_all_pubkeys().

2. peer_try_all_pubkeys does NOT do timing-safe comparison - it iterates through all peers but uses lichen_link_rx() for signature verification. The signature verification (Schnorr-48) in the link layer should be constant-time, but this module doesn't verify that.

IMPACT: Documentation bug, not code bug. The security analysis is correct but the function name is stale.

FIX: Update comment to reference peer_try_all_pubkeys() and verify lichen_link_rx() signature verification is actually constant-time.
close_reason: Updated stale peer_find_locked() timing comment to reference peer_try_all_pubkeys() and current all-peers signature verification flow
